title: Annos comments after The Wind Rises
display_title: Annos comments after The Wind Rises
wikitext:
[]: Anno's comments after The Wind Rises             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jul 29, 2014 4:28 pm Not sure if this is actually worth its own thread, but I think this may be just relevant and interesting enough to be mentioned in Eva context. The following is an exerpt from a conversation between Hideaki Anno and Ghibli producer Toshio Suzuki after the recording was finished (so early 2013, post-Q release). SPOILERS FOR THE WIND RISES SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 29, 2014 4:37 pm Excellent... Definitely makes me feel even more optimistic about Final . Sounds like they'll hit Shinji over the head with that theme in Final moreso than in EoTV/EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 29, 2014 4:44 pm That'll probably be brought about his catatonia and probably desire to cease to exist after the events of Q, they'll probably hammer the fact that simply giving up and killing yourself is taking the easy way out and won't solve any problems, and that if he wants to have a chance to set things right, then he must live.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Tue Jul 29, 2014 4:47 pm I feel like Evangelion (EoTV and EoE) were already the "you must live" kind of message anyway. Though perhaps this time it'll be a more directly "happy" end - like everyone seems to suspect.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jul 30, 2014 3:10 am Meh. It won't be sunshine and Shipping I can tell ya that much. In Eva there are no happy endings, just less sad ones. As long as it's creative and does something different that isn't EOE with CGI I will be happy. I'm not expecting a happy ending for Shinji, but I'm not expecting another Grimdark doom ending either.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jul 30, 2014 4:26 am Let's keep in mind what happened the last time an Evangelion character said anything along the lines of "You must live" to somebody, hmm? ;) SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jul 30, 2014 4:45 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Let's keep in mind what happened the last time an Evangelion character said anything along the lines of "You must live" to somebody, hmm? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jul 30, 2014 4:59 am Not a tie-in like a movie tie-in: more like what Anno said could tie into the fourth Eva movie. I would love to see where Anno runs with this if it turns out to have anything to do with FINAL.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Jul 30, 2014 6:02 am While being a cool tidbit of information, we cannot necessarily project Anno's feelings for Kaze Tachinu to how he wants Eva to be made and perceived. Just saying.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jul 30, 2014 7:16 am ^ Of course not, but since Evangelion was always a very intimist work from Anno, even the Rebuild, and heavily influenced by his state of mind, so his answers in that interview might clue us about his state of mind and thus what direction FINAL will take. Maybe. From what we can see here, Anno is much more optimistic and hopeful than back in the 90's (not hard mind you), yet not naive and knows that even doing what's obviously the right thing (to keep on living) can be very hard and painful, while just ending everything looks so tempting. And that mindset can very well influence the direction FINAL will take, especially since there are some parallels between Shinji and Jiro's story, both good people that wanted a peaceful life, and who were forced to do things that brought forth many sufferings, and ended crushed by guilt and contemplating death. Although of course there are significant differences, in the scope of the suffering (allegedly the whole planet for Shinji, the people killed by the Zeros during the war by Jiro), how much aware they were of what they were doing (Shinji didn't had any clues of what would happen, Jiro knew that his planes would serve to kill people), and for the moment the results of their guilts (Jiro contemplated suicide but finally decided against it, Shinji mentally self-destroyed, with the possibility that he get better later)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 30, 2014 7:21 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Let's keep in mind what happened the last time an Evangelion character said anything along the lines of "You must live" to somebody, hmm? ;) SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Jul 30, 2014 7:29 am ^ I couldn't see Jiro contemplating about anything, really. He was portrayed as a pretty big asshole imho, narrow scope of the impact of his actions, while being fully aware how the situation was. He was well aware that in pursuing his dream he is directly responsible for the death of many people, yet he chose to wilfully neglect how his action would impact others. He was not forced from the outside like Shinji was and (movie-ver.)-Jiro never even intended to repair what he has caused, conveniently walking his path no matter what it caused. Yes, I ♥ The Wind Rises, you can really bite your teeth into this substance, but you can also enjoy it for what it is.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 30, 2014 7:38 am robersora wrote: Yes, I ♥ The Wind Rises, you can really bite your teeth into this substance, but you can also enjoy it for what it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jul 30, 2014 10:53 am I felt like it was the opposite. A story about engineering dreams with a love story in the background. Jiro was a wonderfully complex character. You could tell he loved Nahoko with all his heart but at the same time while she was dying he was still 100% dedicated to his work & his actions could easily be misread as not caring. I feel it's easily Miyazaki's richest film thematically. So glad he didn't end his career on Ponyo which was cute but nothing more.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 30, 2014 11:05 am Well, both aspects matter. But as an engineer, I found the engineering parts of the film not quite as convincing as I'd hoped, so the love side gained in importance for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 30, 2014 11:12 am Ray wrote: Meh. It won't be sunshine and Shipping I can tell ya that much. In Eva there are no happy endings, just less sad ones.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 30, 2014 11:34 am Bagheera wrote: After the End would like a word with you.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jul 30, 2014 12:54 pm I don't count either of those as Canon. I count EOE as the true ending, regardless of how dark it may be its canon and I gotta deal with it. Now as to how this pertains to Rebuild of Evangelion? I've stated it before, I believe there is too much stacked against Shinji for a happy ending to be believable. Again, I doubt he'll die or spend the rest of his life in a padded cell, but asking for a 'happy' ending for Shinji after playing a direct part in the unforgivable crime of Genocide and Gaiacide, with only one 90-120 movie left is likely too much to ask for.Sure 'he must live' but will that life be worth living after everything that's happened? But if all else fails I'm glad to see Anno's pretty much done a 180 in terms of worldview though.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 30, 2014 1:02 pm Ray wrote: I don't count either of those as Canon.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Jul 30, 2014 1:04 pm ^ Then let me as you a simple question Ray: If you acknowledge that Shinji's crime is too massive and "unforgiveable" as you say, why are YOU able to forgive him and wish for him to have a happy life?
